title: 7.3.2 Evaluation Procedures for In-Plane Behavior of Piers
description: A three-step process to determine pier capacity, predict behavior modes, and reconcile findings with observed field damage.

variables.alpha: 0.5 for fixed-free cantilever; 1.0 for fixed-fixed pier
variables.h_eff: Height to resultant of lateral force

variables.v_me: Bond plus friction strength of mortar

diagonal_tension_Vdt.formula: V_dt = f'_dt * A_n * beta * (1 + f_ae / f'_dt)^0.5

variables.beta: 0.67 for L/h_eff < 0.67; 1.0 for L/h_eff > 1.0; interpolated between

toe_crushing_Vtc.formula: V_tc = alpha * P_CE * (L / h_eff) * (1 - f_ae / 0.7 * f'_me)

piers_aspect_ratio_gt_1_25.condition: V_bjs1 is lowest and < 0.75 of V_tc or V_r
V_tc or V_r are lowest and < 0.75 of V_bjs1

piers_aspect_ratio_le_1_25.condition: V_r or V_tc are lowest and f_ae < 100 psi
V_r or V_tc are lowest and f_ae >= 150 psi

step_3_field_reconciliation.guideline: Compare predicted mode with observed field damage. If consistent, classification and capacity are correct.
step_3_field_reconciliation.failure_logic: If field damage is inconsistent, return to Step 1 and vary material property assumptions (e.g., using 1/30 of brick compressive strength for diagonal tension strength).

technical_notes.face_brick_caution: If face brick is unbonded, reduce h/t thickness to backing wythes only.
